summary: Daily Hacker News product scout: 12 concrete items selected from 22 fully crawled qualifying threads.
telegram_footer: 12 items surfaced | 22/22 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Handy
items.source_story_title: Apple's new SpeechAnalyzer API, benchmarked against Whisper and its predecessor
items.summary: Open-source cross-platform dictation app with local Parakeet transcription support.
items.why_included: Several commenters independently recommended it as a practical local transcription application.
items.evidence: Users called Handy “everything I want” and reported reliable daily use with Parakeet v3.

items.name: Axiom
items.source_story_title: Building and Shipping Mac and iOS Apps Without Ever Opening Xcode
items.summary: Open-source Apple-platform agent skills and token-efficient command-line tools.
items.why_included: It directly addresses agent-driven Apple development without depending on Xcode’s GUI.
items.evidence: Its author described tools for logs, profiling, symbols, and UI automation; a commenter called Axiom excellent.

items.name: Sweetpad CLI
items.source_story_title: Building and Shipping Mac and iOS Apps Without Ever Opening Xcode
items.summary: CLI and VS Code tooling for building Swift and iOS apps.
items.why_included: It is a concrete Xcodebuild wrapper aimed at both humans and coding agents.
items.evidence: A commenter reported the beta CLI already pleasant on a real project and linked its open-source repository.

items.name: Logseq DB
items.summary: Local-first outliner and knowledge base with database-backed block organization.
items.why_included: The beta drew detailed real-world feedback on speed, tags, Markdown mirroring, and self-hosted sync.
items.evidence: A year-long nightly user praised fast block tagging and retrieval, while noting remaining rough edges.

items.name: Densha
items.source_story_title: A voxel Tokyo in real Japan time – ride the Yamanote line and study Japanese
items.summary: Real-time voxel Tokyo experience for ambient Japanese listening practice.
items.why_included: It is an unusual language-learning environment with clear community interest despite performance caveats.
items.evidence: Commenters described the Tokyo visuals as pleasant, liked the concept, and explained its listening-practice flow.

items.name: DOM-docx
items.source_story_title: Show HN: DOM-docx – HTML to native, editable Word docs (MIT)
items.summary: MIT-licensed converter from HTML to editable native Word documents.
items.why_included: It targets a persistent document-automation gap: editable DOCX rather than rendered exports.
items.evidence: The author detailed screenshot-based fidelity testing against LibreOffice and iterative OOXML optimization.

items.name: Nobie
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Nobie – an Excel-compatible runtime for agents and humans
items.summary: Rust-built Excel-compatible runtime and CLI for spreadsheets and agents.
items.why_included: It makes existing XLSX models programmable without forcing a new spreadsheet format.
items.evidence: The team described native Mac and Linux CLI support, incremental recalculation, and agent-oriented workbook tools.

items.name: Zork-Bench
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (July 2026)
items.summary: Interactive-fiction benchmark for testing agent runtime, harness, and prompting choices.
items.why_included: It offers a concrete, inspectable environment for experimenting with agent behavior.
items.evidence: A builder shared results and sessions from tuning a harness with Qwen3.6-27B.

items.name: Video Hub App
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (July 2026)
items.summary: Desktop app for browsing, searching, tagging, and rating personal video collections.
items.why_included: It is a long-running, cross-platform utility with a clear purpose outside the usual AI tooling.
items.evidence: Its maker reported an eighth-year update and linked both the app and public source repository.

items.name: Fluxmail MCP
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (July 2026)
items.summary: Self-hosted MCP server that gives agents unified access to email accounts.
items.why_included: It foregrounds privacy-preserving agent email access across standard protocols.
items.evidence: The builder said it supports Gmail plus IMAP/SMTP and multiple accounts without a hosted connector.

items.name: Source Library
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (July 2026)
items.summary: Digital collection of 15,000-plus translated Renaissance and premodern texts.
items.why_included: It is a substantial public-facing research archive with direct API access.
items.evidence: A commenter called the collection exactly what they needed, despite critiquing onboarding friction.

items.name: Narro
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (July 2026)
items.summary: User-curated chronological social feed with no ads, tracking, or infinite scroll.
items.why_included: Its finite-feed design is a concrete alternative to engagement-maximizing social apps.
items.evidence: The builder explained that Narro aggregates followed profiles and deliberately ends after new posts.
